tasks.spec:
You are a build worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave all changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only; match the code style of factory/*.py (module docstring, from __future__ import annotations, argparse main()).

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY these paths (create them): departments/podcast/runtime/sense_estate.py, departments/podcast/runtime/compare_charter.py, departments/podcast/runtime/fingerprint_dedup.py, departments/podcast/runtime/escalate_outbox.py, departments/podcast/runtime/record.py, departments/podcast/runtime/estate.json, departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py. Never modify anything else.

READ FIRST (read-only source material): departments/podcast/procedural-graph.md (SG-WATCHDOG section), departments/podcast/charter.yaml, departments/podcast/knowledge/concept-map.md, factory/manager.py (record-order + atomic-write conventions), factory/human_in_the_loop.py (escalate()), factory/charter_loader.py.

INTERFACE CONTRACT (frozen — every lane builds against this; do not deviate): Observation rows appended to <state-dir>/observations.jsonl: {"ts": ISO8601-UTC, "sensor": one of timer|receipt|log|channel|vps, "subject": str, "status": "ok"|"warn"|"fail"|"unknown", "evidence": path-or-url str, "detail": short str, "metrics": object}. Incidents in <state-dir>/incidents.json, a dict keyed by fingerprint, written atomically (temp + os.replace): {"fingerprint": sha256hex[:12] of 'sensor|subject|failure_class', "failure_class": str, "first_seen": ISO, "last_seen": ISO, "state": "open"|"resolved"|"department_defect", "severity": "critical"|"high"|"med"|"low", "setpoint": str, "observed": value, "evidence": [str], "one_question": str, "count": int}. Record order enforced by record.py: append <state-dir>/runs.jsonl receipt FIRST, then STATE.json (atomic, epoch strictly +1, refuse to write if on-disk epoch >= intended epoch: single-writer), then touch/update <state-dir>/heartbeat. Every node script takes --state-dir (default departments/podcast/state resolved from repo root) and --shadow (DEFAULT ON; --live to disable). Shadow = zero external side effects: no network, no subprocess except read-only systemctl queries.

BUILD these five nodes per SG-WATCHDOG:
1. sense_estate.py — reads estate.json (you author it; see inventory below), senses each unit via a pluggable provider callable (default: read-only 'systemctl --user show <unit> -p ActiveState,SubState,Result,ExecMainStatus' + receipt-file mtime freshness under the receipts dir + log tail error patterns), and appends one Observation per inventory unit. HARD RULE (the silent-gap guard, concept C16): the emitted observation set MUST cover every unit in estate.json — if a probe errors, emit status unknown with the error as detail; a unit absent from output is a defect your own self-check must catch (assert coverage before exit). Also sense escalation-channel liveness as sensor=channel: in shadow this checks only config presence/reachability metadata, NEVER sends anything.
2. compare_charter.py — loads setpoints via factory.charter_loader.load_charter (charter is the source of truth; never hardcode numbers), consumes observations.jsonl, classifies breaches into incident candidates {failure_class, severity, setpoint, observed, one_question} using an enumerable state machine (no heuristics beyond the charter numbers; concept C14).
3. fingerprint_dedup.py — merges candidates into incidents.json: same fingerprint while state=open → increment count + update last_seen, NEVER a second thread (concept C12 part 1); candidate matching a fingerprint in state=resolved → reopen as state=department_defect with one_question explaining the recurrence (concept C12 part 2).
4. escalate_outbox.py — for each NEW open incident (not previously escalated; track escalated fingerprints in incidents.json), call factory.human_in_the_loop.escalate(department='podcast', issue=..., outbox_path=<state-dir>/decisions_outbox.jsonl, context={fingerprint, evidence, one_question}). Shadow: outbox file only, delivered_count==0 externally, and an already-escalated open fingerprint produces NO new outbox row.
5. record.py — the shared receipt writer: takes a node name + payload summary, enforces the record order above. All other nodes call it (import) at the end of their run.
6. estate.json — the sensed inventory. Author it with EXACTLY this data: systemd user timers with expected cadence: podcast-loop-health (daily), podcast-loop-guest-acquisition (daily), podcast-loop-referral-flywheel (daily), podcast-loop-booking-readiness (daily), podcast-loop-production-publish (daily), podcast-loop-sales-handoff (weekly), podcast-loop-proof-improvement (weekly), podcast-prep-sweep (15min), podcast-context-watch (hourly), obe-scheduled-intent-sweeper (daily), obe-draft-bridge (30min), obe-approved-send-executor (10min); receipts_dir /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast/episodes/_loop_receipts; logs_dir /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast/logs; channels: telegram (via VPS bot, config-presence check only in shadow), linear; vps: host 18.211.196.126, services podcast-webhook + podcast-recording-worker, shadow rule: skip ssh entirely and emit status unknown unless --probe-vps is passed. Staleness thresholds: 15-min units stale after 30 min, hourly after 2h, daily after 26h, weekly after 8 days (charter Q4).

TESTS (departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py): fully self-contained under pytest tmp_path with fake providers — NEVER touch the real state dir, real systemctl, or the podcast repo. Required negative tests: (a) a unit present in estate inventory but absent from a fake provider's output still yields an observation (status unknown) — and sense_estate's coverage self-check fails loudly if an observation is dropped; (b) stale receipt (>26h) produces a fail observation; (c) same fingerprint twice while open → one incident, count 2, one outbox row total; (d) resolved fingerprint recurring → state department_defect; (e) record.py refuses an epoch that does not increase; (f) shadow escalation writes outbox rows only (no other side effect observable).

HOW TO RUN: from the worktree root: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py -q — all tests must pass.

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave code uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md (headings: '# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', under 700 words). If blocked, explain in fix-summary.md instead of making speculative edits.
tasks.verified: pytest passed on the spine's own negative-test suite and the exported patch touches only the seven owned spine files

tasks.spec:
You are a build worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave all changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only; match the style of factory/*.py.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: departments/podcast/runtime/pipeline_sensor.py, departments/podcast/runtime/publish_verifier.py, departments/podcast/runtime/manifest_sensor.py, departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_sensors.py. Never modify anything else. Another concurrent lane owns record.py/compare_charter.py — do NOT create or import-require them; your sensors must run standalone (they only APPEND observations).

READ FIRST (read-only): departments/podcast/procedural-graph.md (SG-PIPELINE, SG-PUBLISHDAY, SG-MANIFEST), departments/podcast/charter.yaml, departments/podcast/knowledge/concept-map.md, factory/charter_loader.py, kernel/capabilities.py.

INTERFACE CONTRACT (frozen): append Observation rows to <state-dir>/observations.jsonl: {"ts": ISO8601-UTC, "sensor": "pipeline"|"publishday"|"manifest", "subject": str, "status": "ok"|"warn"|"fail"|"unknown", "evidence": str, "detail": short str, "metrics": object}. Every node takes --state-dir (default departments/podcast/state from repo root), --shadow (DEFAULT ON), and --sources <dir> pointing at fetched source artifacts. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this department runs with NO ambient credentials (kernel/capabilities.py) and sensors must NEVER call live calendar/HubSpot/RSS/YouTube APIs themselves. Each sensor reads provider-fetched JSON artifacts from --sources (the kernel fetch seam supplies them later); a missing/unreadable source file yields status unknown with the missing path as detail — NEVER fabricated data, never a crash.

BUILD:
1. pipeline_sensor.py — computes the recording-pipeline guest count vs the charter pipeline_guests setpoint (load via factory.charter_loader; never hardcode 6). Sources: calendar.json (list of {guest, email, event_type, start_iso}) and hubspot_contacts.json (list of {email, podcast_status, name}). INDEPENDENCE RULE (charter C4): the count derives ONLY from these two sources joined on email/name — never from any loop's self-reported receipt. metrics must include counted_guests: [{guest, source_fields}] naming, for every counted guest, exactly which fields in which source qualified them, so the count is auditable. Emit sensor=pipeline, subject='recording-pipeline', status fail when count < setpoint.
2. publish_verifier.py — for each episode due today per --sources publish_schedule.json ({episodes: [{episode_id, publish_date, expected: {rss_url, youtube_url, social_receipts}}]}), verify PROVIDER-side artifacts fetched into --sources (rss.xml, youtube_status.json, social_receipts.json): the episode entry exists in rss.xml, youtube_status shows public, each expected social receipt is present. A missing receipt is status fail (an incident, never a warning — concept C10/C16). No episodes due today = one ok observation with detail 'no publish due'.
3. manifest_sensor.py — per active guest in --sources guest_manifests.json ({guests: [{guest, email, episode_id, fields: {headshot: {present, source}, links: {...}, bio: {...}, promo_assets: {...}}}]}), compute completeness: metrics {required: [...], present: [...], missing: [...], fallback_available: {field: bool}, completeness_pct}. Emit one observation per guest (subject=guest email), status warn when incomplete, fail only when incomplete AND episode publish is within 7 days per the same file. Unresolved identity (missing/duplicate email) → status unknown + detail 'suppress + review' (charter C15), never a guess.

TESTS (departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_sensors.py): self-contained, tmp_path fixtures only — never the real state dir or network. Must cover: independence (pipeline count changes ONLY via source files; counted_guests cites source fields), below-target → fail, missing source file → unknown not crash, publish receipt missing → fail, manifest completeness math + fallback flags, duplicate-email guest → unknown/suppress.

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_sensors.py -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave code uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words). If blocked, say so there instead of guessing.
tasks.verified: pytest passed on the three sensors' suites (independence, fail-on-missing-receipt, completeness math) and the patch touches only the four owned sensor files

tasks.spec:
You are a build worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave all changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only; match the style of factory/*.py.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: departments/podcast/runtime/heal_select.py, departments/podcast/runtime/heal_apply.py, departments/podcast/runtime/heal_verify.py, departments/podcast/runtime/playbooks.json, departments/podcast/tests/test_heal_lane.py. Never modify anything else. A concurrent lane owns the incident/record modules — do not create or require them; consume incidents.json directly as data.

READ FIRST (read-only): departments/podcast/procedural-graph.md (SG-HEAL), departments/podcast/charter.yaml (immutable_safety_invariants + never list), factory/heal_ladder.py (assert_heal_target_allowed, ImmutableHealError).

INTERFACE CONTRACT (frozen): incidents live in <state-dir>/incidents.json, dict keyed by fingerprint: {fingerprint, failure_class, first_seen, last_seen, state, severity, setpoint, observed, evidence, one_question, count}. Heal receipts append to <state-dir>/heals.jsonl: {"ts": ISO, "fingerprint": str, "playbook": str, "mode": "proposed"|"applied", "commands": [str], "result": "proposed"|"verified"|"failed"|"refused", "detail": str}. Every node takes --state-dir (default departments/podcast/state from repo root) and --shadow (DEFAULT ON).

BUILD:
1. playbooks.json — the versioned heal allowlist. Entries: {"id": str, "matches_failure_class": str, "description": str, "commands": [exact command templates], "heal_target": str, "max_attempts_per_day": int}. Seed with exactly three: restart_user_timer (failure_class timer_failed; command 'systemctl --user restart {unit}'; target runtime_unit), recreate_referral_touch_tracker (failure_class missing_tracker_file; command 'python3 /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast/scripts/ensure_referral_touch_tracker.py'; target local_state_file — note: that script is being authored in a separate round; the playbook entry just names it), rerun_failed_step (failure_class receipt_stale; command 'systemctl --user start {unit}'; target runtime_unit).
2. heal_select.py — matches an open incident's failure_class to exactly one playbook entry. No match → write a heals.jsonl row with result=refused and detail 'unknown pattern — escalate' and exit 0 WITHOUT touching anything (unknown = never improvise, charter Q11). Before selecting, call factory.heal_ladder.assert_heal_target_allowed(heal_target) — an ImmutableHealError means result=refused with the error text (a playbook may NEVER touch an immutable invariant, charter C11).
3. heal_apply.py — SHADOW (default): never executes anything; writes heals.jsonl row mode=proposed, result=proposed, with the fully-rendered command list. LIVE (--live): executes ONLY the rendered allowlisted commands via subprocess (no shell=True), enforcing max_attempts_per_day from a counter in <state-dir>/heal_attempts.json; over the cap → refused + detail. Any command not derivable from a playbook template must be structurally impossible — apply takes a playbook id + params, never raw commands.
4. heal_verify.py — re-checks the incident's evidence (pluggable prober callable for tests; default: re-run the same read-only systemctl/file checks the observation cites), and marks heals.jsonl result=verified only when the condition cleared; otherwise result=failed + detail (a heal without verified clearance is a FAILED heal → escalation feed, charter C12).

TESTS (departments/podcast/tests/test_heal_lane.py): self-contained tmp_path + fake probers; never real systemctl, never the real state dir. Required negative tests: (a) unknown failure_class → refused, nothing executed; (b) playbook with heal_target='autonomy_state' (or any invariant) → refused via ImmutableHealError; (c) shadow apply executes NOTHING (assert via a subprocess monkeypatch that would fail the test if called) yet writes the proposed row; (d) attempts over max_attempts_per_day → refused; (e) verify marks failed when the prober says the condition persists.

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/test_heal_lane.py -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave code uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words). If blocked, say so there instead of guessing.
tasks.verified: pytest passed including the refuse-on-unknown, invariant-refusal, and shadow-executes-nothing negative tests; patch touches only the five owned heal files

tasks.spec:
You are a build worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave all changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only; match the style of factory/*.py and kernel/*.py.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: departments/podcast/runtime/kernel_bridge.py, departments/podcast/runtime/podcast_daily.sh, departments/podcast/tests/test_podcast_kernel_bridge.py. Never modify anything else.

READ FIRST (read-only): kernel/bridge.py (load_kernel signature), kernel/capabilities.py, templates/department_daily.sh.template, departments/podcast/charter.yaml (budget.weekly_ceilings), factory/launch.py header comment.

BUILD:
1. kernel_bridge.py — the thin factory-standard wiring: a get_kernel(state_dir) function that calls kernel.bridge.load_kernel(state_dir, budget_ceilings=<the charter's weekly_ceilings loaded via factory.charter_loader>) and returns it; plus a require_shadow() helper that raises RuntimeError if the charter's autonomy_state is not 'shadow' while any --live flag is requested by a caller (belt-and-suspenders for the shadow-first rule). Keep it under ~80 lines; no logic beyond wiring and the guard.
2. podcast_daily.sh — instantiate templates/department_daily.sh.template for DEPARTMENT=podcast, REPO=/mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory: bash with set -euo pipefail; steps: (1) run the watchdog chain IN SHADOW through the confinement launcher — python3 factory/launch.py --department podcast -- python3 departments/podcast/runtime/sense_estate.py --shadow, then compare_charter.py, fingerprint_dedup.py, escalate_outbox.py in that order (these files are being authored by a concurrent lane — reference them by path exactly as written here; do NOT create them); (2) the manager cycle: python3 factory/manager.py --department podcast --root <repo> --outbox <repo>/state/decisions_outbox.jsonl; (3) human-in-the-loop push per the template. Keep the template's comment discipline; the script must remain shadow-only (no --live anywhere).
3. tests/test_podcast_kernel_bridge.py — self-contained: get_kernel returns an object using the charter's ceilings (monkeypatch load_kernel to capture the kwargs); require_shadow raises when charter says shadow and live is requested; podcast_daily.sh passes 'bash -n' syntax check and contains NO occurrence of "--live".

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/test_podcast_kernel_bridge.py -q && bash -n departments/podcast/runtime/podcast_daily.sh

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words). If blocked, say so there instead of guessing.
tasks.verified: bridge tests passed (charter ceilings wired, shadow guard raises), daily script parses and contains no --live, patch touches only the three owned files

tasks.spec:
You are a test-fixture author on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — create files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave all changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY departments/podcast/tests/fixtures/poisoned/ (create the directory) with exactly six files: unit_missing.json, stale_receipt.json, forged_receipt.json, resolved_fingerprint_recurs.json, non_allowlisted_playbook.json, README.md.

PURPOSE: these are POISONED fixtures for negative-testing a watchdog: each encodes exactly one defect that a QA gate must catch. A gate that cannot fail is decoration — these fixtures prove the gates can fail.

EXACT CONTENT CONTRACT (a validator script checks every rule below — follow them literally):
1. unit_missing.json: {"inventory": [>=3 systemd unit name strings], "observations": [rows]} where each observation row has keys ts (ISO8601 UTC), sensor (one of timer|receipt|log|channel|vps), subject, status (ok|warn|fail|unknown), evidence, detail — and AT LEAST ONE inventory unit has NO observation row (that gap is the poison).
2. stale_receipt.json: {"now": ISO8601, "receipt": {"ts": ISO8601 MORE than 26 hours older than now, "loop": str, "verdict": str}}.
3. forged_receipt.json: {"receipt": {"body": obj, "signature": str}, "expected_signature": str} where signature != expected_signature.
4. resolved_fingerprint_recurs.json: {"incidents": {fp: incident}, "new_observation": {"fingerprint": fp}} — at least one incident has state 'resolved' and new_observation.fingerprint equals that resolved key. Every incident needs keys: failure_class, first_seen, last_seen, state, severity, setpoint, observed, evidence, one_question, count.
5. non_allowlisted_playbook.json: {"playbook_allowlist": [>=2 ids], "heal_request": {"playbook": an id NOT in the allowlist, "fingerprint": str}}.
6. README.md (>=40 words): one paragraph per fixture explaining the poison and which gate must FAIL on it.
Use realistic values drawn from a podcast-pipeline watchdog (units like podcast-prep-sweep.timer, loops like referral-flywheel) — but invent nothing beyond fixture data; these files are test data, not documentation of real state.

HOW TO RUN (self-check before finishing): python3 /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory/ringer/podcast-loop-hardening/checks/validate_fixtures.py — run it from the worktree root; it prints each failure reason; fix until it prints PASS.

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave files uncommitted; also write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words).
tasks.verified: the fixture validator proved each of the five poisons is genuinely encoded (schema + poison-specific assertions) and the patch only adds the fixtures directory
